content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 7, 2016

Christi Hogin, City Attorney
Palos Verdes Estates
1230 Rosecrans Avenue, Suite 110
Manhattan Beach, CA 90266

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-15-238

Dear Ms. Hogin:

This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Palos Verdes Estates City
Councilmember James D. Vandeer regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because the Fair Political Practices Commission does not act as a finder
of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), this letter is based on the
facts presented. Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest
provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common
law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

QUESTION

Does the Act prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the governmental decision on
whether to extend the City of Palos Verdes Estates' Concession Agreement with the Palos Verdes
Beach and Athletic Foundation to operate the Palos Verdes Beach and Athletic Club given that the
Councilmember is on the waitlist to join the Club?

CONCLUSION

No. The Act does not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the decision on
whether to extend the City's Concession Agreement with the Foundation to operate the Club.

FACTS

The Palos Verdes Beach & Athletic Club is a semi-private club operated for the benefit of
the public by the Foundation pursuant to a Concession Agreement with the City. The Foundation is
a nonprofit mutual benefit corporation governed by a Board of Directors elected from the Club's
membership. The City owns the land and improvements underlying the Club.

___________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-15-238
Page No. 2

Club members pay an initiation fee and monthly dues. The Club's membership includes over 630 families, and close to 310 families are on the waitlist to join the Club. Positions on the waitlist are nontransferable. The Councilmember is on the waitlist to join the Club.

The term of the Concession Agreement is nearing its end. The Foundation has the option to extend the term of the Concession Agreement and has exercised it. If the City determines that the Foundation has fulfilled its obligations under the Concession Agreement to date, the term of the Concession Agreement will be extended to June 30, 2026. On January 4, 2016, John C. Cotti, an attorney on your staff, confirmed that the City does not intend to raise or lower the Club's initiation fees or monthly dues through the extension of the Concession Agreement.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making, participating in making, or using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial interest. Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will have a material financial effect on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or on one or more of the official's interests. Section 87103 also sets forth the interests from which a conflict of interest may arise. Of those interests, the only one implicated by the decision on whether to extend the Concession Agreement and the facts presented is the Councilmember's interest in his personal finances.

Foreseeability:

In order to determine if the Councilmember has a conflict of interest under the Act with respect to the decision on whether to extend the City's Concession Agreement with the Foundation, we must consider whether the effect of the decision on the Councilmember's interest in his personal finances would be reasonably foreseeable. Under the Act, an effect on an interest is presumed foreseeable if the interest is explicitly involved in the decision, and an interest is explicitly involved if the interest is a named party in, or subject of, the decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) In this case, the Councilmember's personal finances are not explicitly involved in the decision on whether to extend the Concession Agreement. Therefore, the effect of the decision on the Councilmember's personal finances is reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701(b).)

Based on the facts presented, there is no reasonably foreseeable effect of the decision on the Councilmember's interest in his personal finances. The decision cannot affect the value of the Councilmember's position on the waitlist because a position on the waitlist for the Club is nontransferable. Furthermore, the facts presented do not provide any other potential basis for a reasonably foreseeable effect on the Councilmember's personal finances as a result of the decision in light of the fact that the City does not intend to raise or lower the Club's initiation fees or monthly dues through the extension of the Concession Agreement.²

_______________
² If the decision implicated the amount of the Club's initiation fees or monthly dues, it may be reasonably foreseeable that the Councilmember's future cost to join or be a member of the Club might change, and that change could affect the Councilmember's personal finances if he becomes a member of the Club. If these facts arise you should seek additional advice.

File No. A-15-238
Page No. 3

Thus, we conclude that the Act does not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the
decision on whether to renew the City's Concession Agreement with the Foundation.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Matthew F. Christy
Counsel, Legal Division

MFC:jgl

sections.question:
Does the Act prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the governmental decision on 
whether to extend the City of Palos Verdes Estates' Concession Agreement with the Palos Verdes 
Beach and Athletic Foundation to operate the Palos Verdes Beach and Athletic Club given that the 
Councilmember is on the waitlist to join the Club?
sections.conclusion:
No. The Act does not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the decision on 
whether to extend the City's Concession Agreement with the Foundation to operate the Club.

QUESTION: Does the Act prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the governmental decision on 
whether to extend the City of Palos Verdes Estates' Concession Agreement with the Palos Verdes 
Beach and Athletic Foundation to operate the Palos Verdes Beach and Athletic Club given that the 
Councilmember is on the waitlist to join the Club?

CONCLUSION: No. The Act does not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the decision on 
whether to extend the City's Concession Agreement with the Foundation to operate the Club.
